code:
class Node:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data
        self.next = None

def insert_sorted_circular(head, data):
    new_node = Node(data)

    # Case 1: Empty list
    if head is None:
        new_node.next = new_node  # Points to itself
        return new_node

    # Case 2: New node becomes the new head (smallest element)
    # This also covers inserting into a single-node list when data <= head.data
    if data <= head.data:
        # Find the tail of the list
        current = head
        while current.next != head:
            current = current.next
        # current is now the tail
        current.next = new_node   # Tail points to new_node
        new_node.next = head      # new_node points to the old head
        return new_node           # new_node is the new head
    
    # Case 3: New node goes in the middle or becomes the new largest (but not new head)
    # Iterate to find the correct insertion point
    current = head
    while current.next != head and current.next.data <= data:
        current = current.next
    
    # At this point, new_node should be inserted after `current`.
    # `current.next` will be either:
    #   - The node whose data is greater than `new_node.data`
    #   - The head of the list (if `new_node` is the largest element)
    new_node.next = current.next
    current.next = new_node
    
    return head # Head remains the same in these cases
prompt: Insert in sorted circular linked list yaz.
